title: ICC: The temporal vs the strategic
sub_topics: Office of the Prosecutor as part of ICC question
Russia-Ukraine ICC situation as competing priority

stances.claim: Khan's Appeals Chamber victory is temporary — the ICC may eventually dismiss complaints or arrest warrants for lack of jurisdiction; the prosecutor may pivot to higher-priority situations like Russia-Ukraine rather than waste resources here.
stances.rhetorical_move: outcome prediction grounded in cost-benefit analysis of prosecutor's incentives

stances.claim: The Philippines suffered a temporary defeat but won strategic knowledge: at least two ICC judges would dismiss the suit on jurisdiction, and none of the majority took an opposing stance on the jurisdictional issue.
stances.rhetorical_move: reframing of loss as informational gain (litigation reconnaissance)

stances.claim: The pivotal jurisdictional question is statutory construction: Is Bensouda's 2018 'preliminary examination' an integral part of the ICC's authorized 'preliminary investigation'? Is the Office of the Prosecutor an integral part of the ICC? His short answer to both: No.
stances.rhetorical_move: binary statutory-construction question with declared answer

stances.claim: The Philippine government's procedural error was failing to appeal the September 15, 2021 PTC decision; by appealing only the January 26, 2023 decision, the government waived its right to challenge ICC jurisdiction in this round.

signature_phrases: TEMPORARY, I BELIEVE, WAS THE VICTORY
register_markers: TO ANSWER THE QUESTION, LET US FIRST RECALL
decision_framework_signals: applies temporal-vs-strategic framing to reframe procedural setbacks as informational gains
uses statutory construction (whether preliminary examination = preliminary investigation) as pivot for jurisdictional dispute
frames prosecutor's incentive structure as the operative variable for predicting case continuation
